title: French & English Loanwords in Daily Speech
content: Lebanese Arabic naturally incorporates French and English words in daily speech. This is a unique cultural feature, not a sign of poor Arabic.

title: Social Customs That Shape Language
content: Lebanese culture has deep social customs embedded in everyday language. Understanding these is essential to speaking naturally.

items.label: Hospitality phrases are non-negotiable
items.value: Phrases like تفضل, يسلمو, and عا رأسي are social glue. Refusing food must be done gently and repeatedly before it's accepted.

items.label: Inshallah has registers
items.value: Genuine hope, polite deflection, or "absolutely not" — context and tone are everything.

items.label: Compliment ritual
items.value: When someone compliments something you own, respond: هو إلك (huwwe ilak) — "it's yours!" A ritualistic offer you don't mean literally, but must say.

items.label: الله يرحمو (allah yirḥamu)
items.value: "May God have mercy on him/her" — said whenever a deceased person is mentioned. Never skip this.
